message.content:
@startuml
' Define custom styles to match the original colors and shapes

skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0 ' Hide default icons for cleaner look if needed, but we'll use explicit ones below or rely on visibility markers. Actually let's keep them off since I will manually add symbols in text where appropriate? No - better approach: Use standard UML + styling via skinparams/sprites/colors instead of embedding chars directly into names unless necessary — however given constraints here ("as close as possible"), perhaps best practice is NOT embed Unicode glyphs inside method/field strings because they may not render consistently across all tools; rather define helper functions/macros OR just accept slight deviation from exact glyph appearance while preserving semantic meaning through position/ordering AND color-coding which IS supported natively by plantuml! So final decision -> DO include literal unicode characters INSIDE field/method declarations so output looks visually identical even though technically non-standard syntax-wise BUT still parses correctly under most modern parsers including official one online editor etc.) 

Note also need handle inheritance arrows properly dashed vs solid lines per spec too ! Let’s proceed step-by-step building each component carefully ensuring alignment between visual layout logic flow directionality relationships types correct placement order top-down left-right hierarchy levels maintained throughout entire structure without overlapping elements unnecessarily cluttered presentation style clean professional academic grade quality deliverable expected end user
